Sarnak Lightning Caller  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Blunt Atk Delay: 28
DMG: 15 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Fireburst (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 49
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except ROG NEC WIZ MAG ENC BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Blunt Atk Delay: 28
DMG: 15 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:1H Blunt
Stackable:No

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE

Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50

hehe sarnak LIGHTING caller
yeah i think u get that off the sarnak designer he also drops the wand of furniture arranging

Drops off...
QuoteReply
# Feb 22 2002 at 12:59 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592253544816
2 posts

...Underboss Myli'ki in Chardok. He spawns near Grand Herbalist Makha. He is holding the hammer if he drops it.

I can confirm that the effect really works indoors. Look of the weapon is that of the Truncheon of Doom which is bigger than the Sarnak Warhammer if I remember correctly.

Nynaeva
BdG - Sol Ro
▲ Page top
effect
QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2002 at 11:22 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592756654380
140 posts
Lightning Call
Decrease Hit Points (HP) from 110 (L19) to 122 (L25)
Stuns target


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Classes: Dru (L19)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Skill: Conjuration
Allowable Targets: All

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Range to Target: 200 feet
Pushes Target Back: 2 feet
Throws Target Up: 2 feet

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Resistance Check: Magic + 25

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Mana Required: 80
Spell Duration: Instant
Casting Time: 2.75 seconds
Spell Recovery: 2.50 seconds
Recast Delay: 12.0 seconds

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spell cast on you: Wind and lightning slam into you from above.
Spell cast on someone: Soandso is struck by a shock of lightning.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I have one of these on Xegony. Its visible when he's weilding it. The proc is 122 or 133 DD and works indoors.

QuoteReply

22 posts
I have had both my Walking stick and my Yesha club proc without me hitting so it may be a proc off an miss.
▲ Page top
Chardok will be POPULAR
QuoteReply
# Sep 27 2001 at 5:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592341837046
View User Forum
Scholar
582 posts
This is from the Test server, to be sure, but things like this will make it a MUCH more popular dungeon than now -- it's not a Keyed dungeon and it's within a 2-zone run from 2 port-points, and the Frontier Mountains bind point.

Fury of Air is the level 34 Druid spell added with Velious; it does about 250 points of damage and Stuns as well.
This weapon looks like a good successor to a Knuckleduster for monks... say, Jade Mace in primary + this in secondary, reverse if fighting a mob with a Damage Shield or you really need the Stuns, and the associated taunt as well :-)
▲ Page top
RE: Chardok will be POPULAR
QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2001 at 5:34 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767834244
20 posts
Don't reverse if you "really need the stuns"...your offhand weapon can proc on an attack by your primary hand weapon.

To test:
1) equip two weapons of different types (crush vs. pierce vs. slash), with the proccing one in the offhand.
2) fight with "show your hits" until you get hits only from the primary weapon, and a proc from the offhand, in the same combat round. It may take awhile, but it will happen eventually.

Meowse.
▲ Page top
RE: Chardok will be POPULAR
QuoteReply
# Nov 11 2001 at 4:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1615767548027
47 posts
Aye, that is what's happening, you're just proc'ing off your misses.
You'll notice a definite increase in the proc frequency between having your proc weapon in primary as opposed to secondary.
▲ Page top
RE: Chardok will be POPULAR
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 3:58 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I'm sure he knows that the Offhand can proc.
But your Offhand will attack less often than the main hand, and therefore have less chance to Proc. That's what he meant by switching this to the main hand if you really need the stuns.
▲ Page top
RE: Chardok will be POPULAR
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2001 at 9:44 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245652513
7 posts
I doubt that is whats happening. You need to turn on "your misses" because you dont have to hit the mob for your weapon to proc.. just swing it.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 25 2001 at 6:34 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) turn on or turn off your misses and hits? what the heck is that .. all that switch does is filter out what you see on your window .. liek putting someone on ignore will NOT make them go away .. the "turn off/on" misses and huts has NOTHING to do with a weapon proc'ing or anything like that .. where do you people get this stuff?
RE: Chardok will be POPULAR
QuoteReply
# Dec 05 2001 at 5:23 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592342842224
Scholar
110 posts
Sandarian, they are saying this guy must have already had his misses turned off, meaning if they were on it woulda said he missed and it proc'd. Meaning he still swung.
And a question for people reading this like you (as I imagine you will never be back). Why on earth make a log in just to post one flame? If people annoy you so much, up your filter a bit so only posts regarded as useful by many people show up. Flaming just leads to more flaming (Like the ones I will get for this I am sure) and less good info.
